name: goblin-warren-entrance
summary: The outside approach to a goblin warren — a slope-dug hole or cave mouth, ringed with stolen junk, crude traps, and pointless totems. Small, mean, trash-strewn, watched from peep-holes.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: irregular approach funneling into a hole

primary_palette.note: dug hole into a slope, junk heaped around, a trap on the approach
natural cave mouth crudely defended, spike pit half-covered
massive midden outside the hole — stolen goods, broken gear, bones

story_prompts: watchers in the hole — the goblins know you're coming, the approach is primed
mid-feast — bonfire at the entrance, drunk goblins, sentries asleep
recently attacked — adventurers' bodies tangled in the traps, hole silent
the goblins are hiding from something bigger — they'll parley, for once
a 'warren' that's actually a setup — bait, no goblins, waiting for the party to enter

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor — daylit approach, dark hole, small cookfire possible

anti_patterns: no built walls — the hole is a prop, the slope is the room boundary
the junk is THE theme — stolen rich-people items next to rotten trash
traps should be visible to the player but 'hidden' in-fiction — a warning, not a gotcha
do not make it look orderly — goblins live in chaos, just with more layers than it first appears
keep scale SMALL — goblins build small, low, cramped; nothing human-height if avoidable
